_doc: Canonical reference list of the 8 historical scenarios used by the Scenarios page. These are design-spec FACTS about history, not live model output — peak drawdowns and one-line blurbs are stable academic references. Live model output (per-scenario portfolio impact, MacroTilt allocation, regime read) is computed elsewhere and joined at render time.

scenarios.name: Black Monday ('87)
scenarios.blurb: October 1987: 22.6% single-day drop. Bond vol spiked, yields collapsed, dollar weakened.

scenarios.name: Dot-Com Lead-Up ('00)
scenarios.blurb: March 2000: peak of the Nasdaq bubble. Eight-week window before the rollover.

scenarios.name: Dot-Com Flush ('02)
scenarios.blurb: October 2002: capitulation low after 32-month grind. Tech multiples re-rated by 60%+.

scenarios.name: GFC ('08)
scenarios.blurb: September–November 2008. Lehman, Iceland, AIG. MOVE > 250. Credit spreads to 1,800bp.

scenarios.name: Rate Hikes ('18)
scenarios.blurb: Q4 2018: Powell pivot. SPX drew down 19.8% on rate-cycle fears.

scenarios.name: Covid ('20)
scenarios.blurb: March 2020. Liquidity flush, VIX > 80, MOVE > 160, oil briefly negative.

scenarios.name: Inflation ('22)
scenarios.blurb: 2022: 4×75bp Fed hikes. Bonds and stocks both selling off, real yields up 250bp.

scenarios.name: AI Correction ('24)
scenarios.blurb: Late 2024 AI cohort correction · 18% NASDAQ drawdown.
